news.summary: Shortly after the U.S. Court of International Trade invalidated President Donald Trump's tariffs, another federal judge in Washington blocked his attempt to use emergency powers for trade policy changes. U.S. District Judge Rudolph Contreras, appointed by former President Barack Obama, ruled that Trump cannot impose or adjust tariffs unilaterally under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA). Contreras stated that Trump overstepped his authority by using the law to justify tariffs on foreign goods without Congress' approval. The ruling came after Trump cited trade imbalances and fentanyl imports as national security threats. Contreras emphasized that the case is not solely about tariffs. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Federal judges block Trump's tariffs & emergency tariff powers. US Court of International Trade ruled against tariffs. U.S. District Judge Rudolph Contreras blocked Trump's attempt to impose or adjust tariffs without Congress approval. Trump had cited trade imbalances and fentanyl imports as threats to national security. Judge ruled International Emergency Economic Powers Act doesn't grant authority for tariffs without congressional approval.

The conference attracted numerous professionals and experts, with a total attendance of over 2,000 individuals. Hangzhou Diagens Biotechnology Co., Ltd. showcased its latest advancements and innovations in the field, gaining widespread recognition and interest. The company's presence at the conference marked a notable moment for international collaboration and knowledge exchange in genetics research.
news.headline: China's Hangzhou Diagens Biotechnology wowed at ESHG 2025 in Milan with Rapid AI Karyotype Total Solution.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, following a 3.5% contraction in 2020. This growth is mainly being driven by strong recoveries in major economies like the United States and China. The report also highlights that advanced economies are expected to grow by 5.8%, while emerging and developing economies are forecasted to grow by 6.3%. However, this growth is not evenly distributed, as low-income countries are facing significant challenges with only a 2.9% growth rate projection. Moreover, global trade volume is anticipated to increase by 8.4% in 2021 after a 5.3% decline in the previous year. This positive outlook is contingent on the successful distribution of vaccines and continued policy support.
news.headline: Heineken collaborates with a jinxed supporter for 2025 UEFA Champions League final's success, taking drastic measures.

news.headline: Infinix GT VERSE merges power, play, portability for immersive gaming experience as global gaming population reaches 3 billion.

news.summary: A well-known vector database on GitHub is driving numerous AI applications globally. Zilliz, the developer behind the popular open-source vector database Milvus, revealed that Milvus has received 35,000 stars on GitHub. This milestone solidifies Milvus' position as one of the most favored vector databases in the AI community.
news.headline: Milvus reaches 35K GitHub stars, becoming the top open-source vector database for AI, used in thousands of apps worldwide.

news.summary: Renowned economist Nouriel Roubini, also known as "Dr. Doom" for his accurate prediction of the 2008 financial crisis, recently expressed concerns about President Donald Trump's economic decisions. Roubini took to X to share his latest Project-Syndicate column outlining Trump's options and the potential paths for the U.S. economy. In his tweet, Roubini proposed two scenarios: Trump shifting focus from inflationary measures to pro-growth strategies, or facing financial stress leading to a recession and the Republican Party losing the 2026 midterm elections. Roubini highlighted that while policies like tech investment may enhance growth, others could fuel inflation and economic instability. For detailed insights, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Economist Nouriel Roubini warns markets can curb Trump’s economic policies, predicting Republican defeat or market blowback.

news.summary: Pascal St-Jean, the President and CEO of digital asset manager 3iQ Corp., shared the company's ambitious three-phase strategy aimed at enhancing cryptocurrency adoption and fortifying network infrastructure. During an interview with Benzinga, St-Jean outlined the plan, with the initial phase focusing on bolstering adoption and network security via staking and supporting mining infrastructure, particularly through exchange-traded funds. In Phase 2, the company aims to attract more institutional investors by providing educational resources and collaborating with banks and asset managers worldwide. St-Jean alluded to further developments in the strategic plan, discussing the potential expansion and advancements in the digital asset realm. For more details, access the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: 3iQ's CEO Pascal St-Jean reveals three-phase master plan to increase crypto adoption. Solana ETF gains 31% since launch.

news.summary: Singapore-based company Alchemy Pay, a prominent fiat-crypto payment gateway, revealed on May 30, 2025, a significant partnership with World Liberty Financial Inc. (WLFI), a decentralized finance platform. The collaboration aims to enable effortless access to USD1, WLFI's stablecoin linked to the US Dollar. This integration showcases Alchemy Pay's commitment to enhancing the accessibility and usability of cryptocurrency, particularly within the decentralized finance sector.
news.headline: Alchemy Pay integrates with World Liberty Financial to offer access to USD1 stablecoin, expanding options for users.

news.summary: In New York, Tamboran Resources Corporation (NYSE: TBN, ASX: TBN) has concluded their Private Placement Transaction First Tranche, issuing a total of 2,180,515 Common Stock within the company's placement capacity under Listing Rule 7.1. Details of the new shares issuance have been submitted in an Appendix 2A on the ASX. The following Second Tranche of the Placement is contingent on shareholder approval, which will be requested at a Special Shareholder Meeting of the Company scheduled to be held.
news.headline: Tamboran successfully closes First Tranche of Private Placement with 2,180,515 shares issued, Second Tranche awaits shareholder approval.

news.summary: On May 30, 2025, Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson (NASDAQ: ERIC) has a total of 3,371,351,735 shares, with 261,755,983 A shares and 3,109,595,752 B shares. The combined number of votes is 572,715,558.2. The A shares hold a significant portion of these votes, contributing to the company's overall voting power. This information was disclosed by the company based in Stockholm, pointing to the distribution of ownership and voting rights within the organization.
news.headline: Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson has 3.37B shares (261.76M A shares, 3.11B B shares) with 572.7M votes as of May 30, 2025.

news.summary: Starbucks Corp (NASDAQ: SBUX) is making changes to its loyalty program in an effort to improve profit margins and reposition itself as a premium brand. Starting June 24, the company will no longer offer a 25-star bonus for customers using personal reusable cups. Instead, customers will now receive double stars on entire orders. The previous system, introduced in 2022, allowed customers to earn points towards free items at the 100-point redemption level or premium offerings requiring 200-400 stars. With the new structure, customers ordering single beverages will receive fewer stars. This move is part of CEO Brian Niccol's strategy to enhance the company's profitability.
news.headline: Starbucks is cutting its 25-star bonus for reusable cups, shifting to double stars on entire orders to boost profits.

news.summary: On May 29, Bitcoin ETFs in the US saw a significant shift in investor activity as they experienced a net outflow of $347 million, breaking a 10-trading-day streak of inflows. Interestingly, the fund managed by BlackRock was the sole product to record a net inflow during this period.
news.headline: Bitcoin ETFs saw their first combined outflow in 2 weeks, as US ETFs experienced a $347M net outflow on May 29, except for BlackRock which had a net inflow.
